techniques.name: Discern general intelligence requirements
techniques.description: The handler may plan the main objectives of the HUMINT operation they are about to conduct, which refer to the general intelligence requirements of the agency even in the preliminary stages of a HUMINT operation. These requirements are fundamental as they define what information the intelligence agency seeks to gather in a broader theater of operations. By identifying these needs, handlers can effectively plan and prioritize the objectives of the operation. The intelligence requirements typically align with the strategic goals of the agency and provide a framework that guides the entire intelligence-gathering process. The requirements may be assessed regarding their feasibility (if a requirement “can be answered with the available time and resources”), completeness (if the requirement “contains all the specifics needed for collection”) and necessity (if a requirement has not already been answered and registered)70. Intelligence requirements must consider the timeliness, that is, when the required information must be received by the superior. This technique differs from UT0103.001 - Develop strategy: Identify collection requirements in the fact that UT0103.001 refers to a tactic level (for example, the source needs to know the dates the person of interest is in the country or how often they visit), while UT0101 refers to the general needs of the operation (using the same example, to locate a specific individual person of interest).

techniques.name: Conduct general research
techniques.description: The handler may conduct general research previous to the selection of a source, though this research may be updated during the course of the operation.

techniques.name: Conduct general research: Previous reports
techniques.description: The handler may use the information contained in previous reports about the targeted organization, group, area or person of interest.

techniques.name: Conduct general research: Legal aspects
techniques.description: The handler may research the legal aspects of the actions to be conducted on the operation. This includes not only the intelligence agency country's law, but also any other state's laws than need to be taken into account.

techniques.name: Conduct general research: Current situation
techniques.description: The handler may need to get a hold of the current situation, both friendly and enemy, in order to plan the current HUMINT operation.

techniques.name: Conduct general research: Cultural situation
techniques.description: The handler may need to conduct general research about the cultural situation of the targeted organization, group, area or person of interest.

techniques.name: Develop strategy
techniques.description: The handler may develop a strategy around which the operation will be based. This strategy requires identifying the requirements, criteria and resources of different aspects of the operation and, thus, it is a technique that should be considered when planning and selecting the techniques of the tactic UT07 - Impact.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Identify collection requirements
techniques.description: The handler may identify specific informational needs that must be satisfied by the chosen source. This technique differs from UT0101 - Discern general intelligence requirements in the fact that UT0101 refers to the general needs of the operation (for example, to locate a specific individual person of interest), while UT0103.001 refers to a tactic level (using the same example as the previous one, the source needs to know the dates the person of interest is in the country or how often they visit). Collection requirements will depend on the time available.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Identify screening criteria
techniques.description: The handler may identify specific screening criteria, that is, preliminary criteria to guide the screening process in order to identify the source type which will most likely have access to the information needed to complete the collection requirements and the general operational objectives. For instance, if the objective is to gather insider information about cybersecurity threats within a corporation, the handler would establish criteria such as targeting individuals with job titles like "IT Manager" or "Cybersecurity Analyst" on professional networking sites. They may also consider factors such as the individual's history of posting about relevant topics, their connections within the industry, and their participation in online forums or groups focused on cybersecurity.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Identify threats and risks
techniques.description:
The handler may identify the threats and risks that conducting the operation may entail, including those affecting the handlers, the team, the agency and the country.

For instance, if the operation involves engaging with a suspected terrorist cell, the handler would evaluate the risks to themselves, their team, and the broader agency, including exposure of the operation, legal repercussions, and potential retaliation. They would assess the likelihood of being tracked or traced back to their real identities and plan accordingly to mitigate these risks, ensuring robust cyber security measures and employing anonymous communication tools to protect the operatives and their country’s interests.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Identify technical control requirements
techniques.description: The handler may identify technical control requirements, meaning the technical management of sensitive data regarding the source and the operation.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Adapt strategy
techniques.description: The handler may adapt the initially designed strategy after the screening process, the receival of new information or the imposition of new requirements, among others.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Select handler/s
techniques.description: The handler may choose one or more specific handlers that will be charged with the communication with the source or sources and any other requirements specified by the agency.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Select other personnel
techniques.description: The handler may identify and select other personnel needed to conduct the operation. This may include but it is not limited to profilers, screeners or experts.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Distribute resources
techniques.description: The handler may identify the resources needed in order to conduct the operation and will then distribute them as needed.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Identify operation limitations
techniques.description: The handler may identify the specific limitations of the operation. Thus, the handler and their team must understand the constraints associated with the operational environment, the capabilities of the intelligence collection methods and tools being used and the available resources. A proper identification of the restrictions that the handler may face during the operation is key to adapting the plan to the environment in order to ensure that the collection requirements are being met.

techniques.name: Develop strategy: Coordinate with other units
techniques.description: The handler may need to coordinate with other units or agents while planning the operation or during the course of it. Although the segmentation of units and information is key to intelligence agencies, there may be cases in which some of the information required by the HUMINT handler may already have been obtained by other units, or vice versa.

techniques.name: Discern termination criteria
techniques.description: The handler may need to define under which criteria a HUMINT operation should be terminated. Termination criteria for a HUMINT operation are vital to ensure that resources are not wasted and that operational integrity is maintained. These criteria help determine when an operation should be concluded, either due to the fulfillment of its objectives, the reduction of benefits from continuing the operation or other strategic reasons. Identifying specific potential termination criteria at the planning phase can save time from being wasted in later phases. This technique is related with the execution of those in UTA09 - Exit, thus this being part of the planning of said phase.

techniques.name: Discern termination criteria: Uncooperative source
techniques.description: The handler may terminate an operation if the source becomes uncooperative. This could occur due to a change in the source's circumstances, a shift in their loyalty or motivation, or increased risk of exposure. Handlers should be able to recognize when continued efforts with an uncooperative source may lead to diminishing returns or compromise the operation’s security.

techniques.name: Discern termination criteria: Useless source
techniques.description: The handler may identify that a source is no longer useful for the achievement of the intelligence requirements. A source may initially seem valuable but could turn out to be useless due to providing inaccurate, outdated or irrelevant information. It's crucial for handlers to identify shortcuts beforehand to assess the quality and relevance of the information provided by the source. Identifying potential ways of confirming the accuracy in the planning phase helps with the assessment on whether an operation should be terminated to redirect resources more effectively to other sources.

techniques.name: Discern termination criteria: Source incapacity
techniques.description: The handler may terminate the operation if the source is no longer capable of providing information due to incapacitation. This could be due to physical, psychological or social factors that prevent the source from further participation in the operation. Recognizing these signs early helps in time-saving efforts in the pursuit of the operation’s objectives.

techniques.name: Discern termination criteria: Reached goals
techniques.description: The handler may terminate the operation once the specific objectives have been achieved. Continuing the operation beyond this point can lead to unnecessary risks and expenditure of resources. Clear definition and recognition of goal completion are helpful for timely termination and keep resource efficiency.

techniques.name: Discern termination criteria: Changes in strategy
techniques.description:
The handler may need to prolong over a long period of time a relationship until strategic changes justify that the long-term nature of the relationship no longer exists. This could be due to changes in geopolitical situations, agency priorities, or achievement of long-term intelligence goals, but also because of the specific personal environment of the source.

Regular reviews of strategic interests and priorities as well as source’s interests and worries are essential to determine the continued relevance of such operations in the short term.

techniques.name: Discern termination criteria: Handler implication
techniques.description: The handler may feel personally involved with a source beyond professional limits which can critically undermine the operation's integrity and its outcomes. This criterion for termination is vital to maintain operational security and ethical standards. It is important to anticipate when possible potential risks of personal implication as well as indicators that may be announcing these behaviors. This will require the immediate reevaluation, and potentially, the termination of the operation or a change in the handler if the handler becomes personally implicated with the source in a manner that could affect judgment or operational decisions.

techniques.name: Identify potential adversaries
techniques.description: The handler may analyze and identify entities or individuals who might pose a threat to the operation’s success. This involves obtaining information on known adversaries and analyzing patterns that could indicate potential threats. This technique is needed for preemptive risk management, allowing the handler to prepare adequate countermeasures and strategic responses.

techniques.name: Assess adversary’s physical competences
techniques.description: The handler may evaluate the physical capabilities of identified adversaries, focusing on their ability to influence, monitor, or disrupt operations through non-digital means.

techniques.name: Assess adversary’s physical competences: Sophistication
techniques.description: The handler assesses the level of sophistication in the adversary’s operational tactics and techniques in real life scenarios, including counter surveillance capabilities. This includes evaluating the complexity and execution of their previous operations, their ability to carry out covert actions, and their use of advanced physical surveillance or sabotage methods.

techniques.name: Assess adversary’s physical competences: Resources
techniques.description: The handler examines the physical resources available to the adversary, such as manpower, geographical reach, access to critical infrastructures and logistical support, including, but not limited to, capabilities to conduct legal actions and to use state means to influence companies and organizations under the geographical location.

techniques.name: Assess adversary’s cybercapabilities
techniques.description: The handler may assess the cybercapabilities of the adversary, focusing on their ability to take part in digital espionage, cyberattacks and other forms of cyber operations that could threaten the security of the HUMINT operation.

techniques.name: Assess adversary’s cybercapabilities: Sophistication
techniques.description: The handler may evaluate how advanced the adversary’s cyber operations are, considering their known exploits, the complexity of their attack vectors, and their proficiency in bypassing cybersecurity measures. This includes analyzing past cyber incidents attributed to the adversary and assessing their capability to orchestrate sophisticated, targeted cyberattacks.

techniques.name: Assess adversary’s cybercapabilities: Resources
techniques.description: The handler may investigate the resources at the adversary’s disposal in the cyber domain, including their access to hacking tools, availability of skilled personnel, financial backing for cyberoperations, and their collaborations with other cyberentities. This assessment helps in understanding the breadth and depth of the adversary’s potential to mount sustained cyber operations.

techniques.name: Identify sources that need to be screened
techniques.description: The handler may identify sources that need to be screened as part of the planning phase of the HUMINT operation. Screening refers to the process of obtaining information about them in order to evaluate and select those that could be a better match regarding the operation requirements. This process involves identifying both the direct source, the individual who has access to the information required by the handler, and the different indirect or secondary sources, which are individuals that have access to the direct source. Indirect sources can be approached if the handler assesses that the direct source can not be approached because it would pose a high risk for them, the operation or the agency.

techniques.name: Identify sources that need to be screened: Direct source
techniques.description: The handler may identify direct sources that need to be screened as part of the planning phase of the HUMINT operation. Direct sources are individuals who possess the information sought by the intelligence agency.

techniques.name: Identify sources that need to be screened: Indirect source
techniques.description:
The handler may identify indirect sources that need to be screened as part of the planning phase of the HUMINT operation. Indirect sources are individuals who may not possess the desired information themselves but have access to individuals or resources that do. This can include family members, colleagues, or associates of the direct source. Screening indirect sources involves evaluating their relationship with the direct source, their potential influence, and their ability to facilitate access to information. Identifying indirect sources that need to be screened allows the handler to expand their network and gather intelligence through secondary channels.

The handler is ensuring the protection of communication channels, data storage, and overall operational security (OPSEC) to support the operation's requirements. Given the cyber component of virtual HUMINT, technical safeguarding techniques regarding the protection of operational infrastructure must not be confused with general cybersecurity measures. The former involves implementing specific security practices essential for the agency’s operational integrity, while the latter may refer to broader IT security efforts. This focused safeguarding includes advanced encryption practices, secure handling and transmission of data, and counter-surveillance techniques. These measures are designed to prevent unauthorized access and leaks, ensuring that sensitive information is transmitted and stored securely to support the strategic objectives of the operation.

techniques.name: Use encrypted communications
techniques.description: The handler may secure all communications using robust encryption methods to ensure that any intercepted messages remain inaccessible to unauthorized parties. This practice is critical for maintaining the confidentiality and integrity of operational exchanges, safeguarding them against eavesdropping and interception by adversaries.

techniques.name: Use unencrypted communications
techniques.description: The handler may strategically use unencrypted communications to send non-sensitive information. This may serve as a decoy. This technique is used sparingly and judiciously to avoid actual data compromise while potentially diverting attention from more critical, encrypted channels.

techniques.name: Use encrypted devices
techniques.description:
The handler may implement encryption across all operational devices to mitigate the risks associated with data exposure in the event of loss or theft. This technique is critical not only for maintaining the confidentiality of the data but also for preserving the integrity and availability of information crucial to the operation.

Linux devices. Implement encryption using LUKS (Linux Unified Key Setup), which is integrated with the system's volume management. This method offers strong encryption that secures the entire disk, including swap spaces and temporary files, ensuring that all aspects of the device are protected.

MacOS devices. Implement encryption using FileVault to provide full-disk encryption, which automatically encrypts the entire system drive and any files added to it, securing the device with powerful user authentication during startup.

Windows devices, BitLocker is the standard encryption tool provided by Microsoft. It offers full-disk encryption, which encrypts the entire drive where Windows and user data reside. BitLocker integrates with the Trusted Platform Module (TPM) hardware on the device to ensure that the encryption keys are stored securely and are only accessible upon successful authentication.

iOS devices. iOS uses built-in encryption that is automatically enabled when a user sets a passcode.

Android devices. Adopt full-disk encryption using AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) or file-based encryption, depending on the version of the operating system. Newer versions of Android support file-based encryption which allows encrypted files to be individually locked or unlocked, providing more granular access controls alongside traditional full-disk encryption methods.

Other operating systems and devices may be added depending on the operational needs. In any case, participants should be trained and guided to use them safely.

techniques.name: Use file encryption
techniques.description: The handler may implement file-specific encryption, opting to encrypt individual files rather than the entire operating system. This targeted encryption allows for greater control over which data needs higher levels of security, reducing resource usage and enhancing access to nonsensitive data. This is different from UT0403 - Use encrypted devices because this technique was focused on the encryption of the whole device while UT0404 - Use file encryption is focused on the encryption of specific files or information.

techniques.name: Send encrypted content
techniques.description: The handler may transmit information using encrypted formats to protect the content's confidentiality during transmission. This ensures that sensitive data, such as operational plans or intelligence reports, is shielded from potential interception.

techniques.name: Send encrypted content: Symmetric encryption
techniques.description: The handler may use symmetric encryption techniques for sending encrypted content where the same key is used for both encryption and decryption. This method is efficient for scenarios where secure channels for key exchange exist, offering fast and reliable security for continuous data exchange.

techniques.name: Send encrypted content: Asymmetric encryption
techniques.description: The handler may use asymmetric encryption (e. g., PGP) to send content. In asymmetric encryption two different keys are required for encryption and decryption. This technique is vital for secure communication over unsecured networks, where key exchange might otherwise compromise the encryption.

techniques.name: Send one-view-only content
techniques.description: The handler may send messages or documents that are designed to be viewed once before becoming inaccessible. This technique is used for highly sensitive information that must be tightly controlled and minimized for exposure risk.

techniques.name: Manual content edition
techniques.description: The handler may need to edit previously sent contents, either textual or multimedia. This may be used as a way of hiding contents sent by mistake or removing artifacts that exposed information which was not expected to be sent or that showed information wanted to be edited. Many messaging applications ensure that this information is edited at both ends of the conversation. However, depending on the sophistication of the source, it has to be assessed whether it is possible that he/she has technical knowledge to track edited content. Note that editing content sent by mistake may expose the information even if the source has not seen it after the edition. A typical scenario when this can happen is when using mobile messaging apps such as WhatsApp and Telegram. These applications can be configured to show a notification in the lock screen which is shown at the moment of receiving the message in the application and the message may not be updated when the edition takes place, revealing the original message only in the lock screen. Note that some applications like Signal may still show edit history as well as the edited disclaimer showing previous versions of the message.

techniques.name: Manual content deletion
techniques.description: The handler may need to delete previously sent contents, either textual or multimedia. This may be used as a way of hiding contents sent by mistake or removing artifacts that exposed information which was not expected to be sent or that showed information that should be deleted. Many messaging applications ensure that this information is edited at both ends of the conversation. However, depending on the sophistication of the source, they have to be assessed whether it is possible that they have technical knowledge to track edited content. Note that editing content sent by mistake may expose the information even if the source has not seen it after the edition. A typical scenario when this can happen is when using mobile messaging apps such as WhatsApp and Telegram. These applications can be configured to show a notification in the lock screen which is shown at the moment of receiving the message in the application and the message may not be updated when the edition takes place, revealing the original message only in the lock screen. Note that messages displayed as notifications may still be recoverable in a forensic investigation of the device.

techniques.name: Scheduled message deletion
techniques.description: The handler may set up communications to auto-delete based on a pre-set schedule, ensuring that sensitive information does not remain accessible beyond its useful life. Note that deleting messages from a device may harden the way in which this information is obtained later and will not be as reachable as expected under certain circumstances.

techniques.name: Scheduled message deletion: Manual
techniques.description: The handler may manually delete messages or documents either on a regular basis or under specific circumstances.

techniques.name: Scheduled message deletion: Automated
techniques.description: The handler may use automated systems or application configurations to manage the deletion of application messages and/or contents, ensuring consistency and reliability in the removal of sensitive content without requiring manual intervention.

techniques.name: Hide technical location
techniques.description: The handler may mask the technical location (mainly, source IP address) of operations to prevent geographical tracking and enhance operational security. Especially when dealing with advanced state adversaries that may also have access to Internet Service Providers.

techniques.name: Hide technical location: Proxy
techniques.description: The handler may use proxy servers to obscure their IP addresses, effectively masking the actual location and network details from observers and potential interceptors.

techniques.name: Hide technical location: VPN
techniques.description: The handler may use Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) to securely connect to the internet, providing a safe tunnel for data and disguising the real IP address behind a server located in a different geographic location.

techniques.name: Hide technical location: Layered networks
techniques.description: The handler may use layered networks like Tor 71 or I2P72 to provide multi-layered encryption and routing through multiple nodes, significantly obscuring the origin and nature of the traffic.

techniques.name: Hide technical location: Application settings
techniques.description: The handler may configure applications to hide the IP address or location information from contacts, adjusting settings within the app to enhance anonymity and reduce digital footprints.

techniques.name: Use privacy-preserving operating systems
techniques.description:
The handler may adopt operating systems designed specifically for privacy and security, which come equipped with tools and configurations that enhance the protection of operational data

techniques.name: Voice distorter
techniques.description: The handler may use voice distortion technology during voice communications to prevent voice recognition and protect the identity of operatives.

techniques.name: Video filters
techniques.description: The handler may apply video filters during video communications to obscure physical features or backgrounds that could reveal the location or identity of operatives.

techniques.name: Use cryptocurrencies
techniques.description: This TTP involves the strategic use of cryptocurrencies to conduct financial transactions that are necessary for operational activities while maintaining a high degree of anonymity and operational security. Cryptocurrencies, due to their decentralized nature and potential for nontraceable transactions, serve as an effective tool in operations that require confidentiality and financial discretion.

techniques.name: Use cryptocurrencies: KYC exchanges
techniques.description: This technique involves using cryptocurrency exchanges that make use of Know Your Customer (KYC) procedures. These exchanges typically demand users to provide identification and other personal information to comply with regulatory requirements. Despite the loss of anonymity, using KYC exchanges may be strategically beneficial for handlers who need to access large amounts of cryptocurrency or interact with specific financial networks and can be considered an option depending on the resources of the adversary. Handlers may use these platforms when the operation requires access to a wide range of financial services or when the operational cover aligns with the legitimacy of such exchanges. However, handlers must carefully manage the identity and information provided during the KYC process to avoid compromising the operation.

techniques.name: Use cryptocurrencies: Non-KYC exchanges
techniques.description:
In contrast to KYC exchanges, non-KYC exchanges do not require users to provide personal identification information. These exchanges allow the handler to maintain a higher degree of anonymity when conducting cryptocurrency transactions. Non-KYC exchanges are often preferred in operations where maintaining operational security and avoiding linkages to realworld identities is critical. The handler can use these platforms to acquire, trade, or convert cryptocurrencies without leaving a traceable identity behind. However, these exchanges may have limitations on transaction volumes or the range of supported currencies, which must be considered during operational planning.

https://tails.net/.

techniques.name: Use cryptocurrencies: Buy virtual assets
techniques.description: The handler may use cryptocurrencies to purchase virtual assets and gift cards that can be leveraged in operational activities or used to be transferred directly to sources as a payment. Virtual assets may include digital goods, online services, or virtual real estate, among others. Also, these assets can be instrumental in establishing and maintaining operational covers or facilitating communication and coordination in a covert manner. By using cryptocurrencies for such purchases, the handler ensures that these transactions are conducted with a level of anonymity that reduces the risk of exposure. The use of virtual assets in operations may also help in disguising the nature of financial flows, making it more difficult for adversaries to discern the true purpose of the transactions.

techniques.name: Use cryptocurrencies: P2P physical exchanges
techniques.description: The handler may conduct peer-to-peer (P2P) cryptocurrency exchanges in a physical, face-toface setting. In this method, the handler meets with another individual in person to exchange cryptocurrency for cash or other assets, completely bypassing online platforms. This approach is highly advantageous for maintaining operational security and anonymity, as it eliminates digital traces that could be monitored or recorded by adversaries or surveillance systems. Physical P2P exchanges are often conducted in discrete locations to ensure privacy and reduce the risk of interception or observation. The handler must carefully select trustworthy counterparts to avoid scams or potential security threats during the transaction. Additionally, this method allows for the exchange of cryptocurrencies without the need for intermediaries, KYC processes, or any online interaction, significantly enhancing the operation’s security by reducing exposure to digital surveillance. By utilizing physical P2P exchanges, the handler can acquire, dispose of or trade cryptocurrencies with minimal risk of traceability, ensuring that the financial aspect of the operation remains covert and secure.

techniques.name: Use cryptocurrencies: P2P online exchanges
techniques.description: Peer-to-peer (P2P) cryptocurrency online exchanges enable direct transactions between individuals without the need for an intermediary. This method allows the handler to buy, sell or trade cryptocurrencies directly with other users, often with minimal or no identity verification. P2P exchanges are advantageous for maintaining operational security and anonymity since they typically offer greater flexibility in terms of transaction terms and may support various payment methods. The handler can use P2P exchanges to acquire cryptocurrencies in a decentralized manner, making it harder for adversaries to track or block the financial resources necessary for the operation.

techniques.name: Use cryptocurrencies: Mixing services
techniques.description: The handler may use cryptocurrency mixing services to further obscure the origins and destinations of cryptocurrency transactions. Mixing services scramble operations conducted by multiple users, breaking the clear trace of funds from sender to receiver. This method disperses transaction details across a wide array of unrelated transactions, making it incredibly difficult for observers to trace the funds' original source or eventual destination. By using mixing services, the handler adds an additional layer of anonymity to cryptocurrency transactions, enhancing the confidentiality and security of financial operations critical to the mission’s success.

techniques.name: Use cryptocurrencies: Privacy aware cryptocurrencies
techniques.description: The handler may opt to use privacy-focused cryptocurrencies, such as Monero 76, Zcash and Dash, which offer built-in anonymity features that obscure transaction details to a greater extent than more transparent blockchain technologies like Bitcoin 77. These cryptocurrencies use various methods to ensure that all (in the case of Monero78) or part of the transactions (in the case of Zcash and Dash) are not traceable and remain confidential. By leveraging these privacy-aware cryptocurrencies, the handler ensures that financial transactions related to the operation leave minimal digital footprints, significantly reducing the risk of exposure and tracking by adversaries.

techniques.name: Hide notifications preview
techniques.description:
The handler may configure all operational devices to hide notification previews on lock screens and notification centers. This measure ensures that sensitive information is not exposed inadvertently when devices are unattended or accessed by unauthorized individuals. By preventing the display of message content in notifications, the handler reduces the risk of accidental data leakage, maintaining the confidentiality and integrity of communication.

The handler is trying to obtain information about the possible sources in order to evaluate them and select those that could be a better match regarding the operation requirements. This tactic includes actions regarding the profiling of the sources and the identification of their baseline if possible, as well as the selection of one or more sources. Though in some manuals the concept of screening is exclusively used to refer to the process of identifying a source's access to the information and willingness to collaborate, in this model it also includes the profiling of a source, the identification of their baseline and the action of choosing an individual. Although the process of screening sources can be done in parallel with the collection of information, it is advised to be done previously in order to both it be done adequately and to subsequently tailor the strategy to the chosen source. Moreover, the screening process may be carried out by a professional other than the final handler, namely the screener or profiler, which will in turn subsequently pass the profile information onto the handler or the handler's team.

techniques.name: Profile the possible sources
techniques.description: The handler may profile the different possible sources, that is, to conduct a systematic gathering and analysis of information about said individual. The profiler's goal is to understand their backgrounds, personality, behaviors, motivations and potential vulnerabilities. This technique may be conducted by the same handler that will approach the source or by a selected professional with profiling skills.
